incidents.summary: Jarvis worker OpenCode stalls were failing host no-output timeout correctly but the same dispatch message was being automatically retried, causing repeat respawns of the same run_id instead of a terminal fail-loud outcome.
notes: Confirmed live on run jarvis-nan-54-probe-1773664524945-378xt.
Host watchdog fix landed in src/container-runner.ts: synthetic worker-opencode-active heartbeats no longer re-arm no-output timeout.
Retry policy fix landed in src/index.ts with src/worker-agent-error-policy.ts: worker timeout errors are treated as terminal and should not be automatically retried.
Pending verification: timeout of the replayed run under the new runtime must stay terminal and avoid another respawn.

2026-03-16 reliability hardening: andy-developer now defers older review backlog behind fresh intake, review requests force a fresh Andy session, and fresh-intake preemption escalates from close sentinel to bounded force-stop if the stale session does not yield.

2026-03-16 worker shutdown hardening: single-shot worker agent-runtime now writes a graceful _close sentinel first and only falls back to container stop after a grace window, reducing normal post-completion exit 137 dependence.

2026-03-16 worker confidence: live probes reached review_requested on both jarvis-worker-1 (probe-jarvis-worker-1-1773688332-4691) and jarvis-worker-2 (probe-jarvis-worker-2-1773687505-30281); verify-worker-connectivity now passes.
2026-03-16 ops signal cleanup: preflight/status now report recent successful worker evidence instead of misclassifying quiet lanes as inactive from chat message timestamps alone.

incidents.summary: Feature catalog strict validation is failing because several legacy docs and launchd references in the seed catalog no longer exist, which weakens the feature-tracking gate for docs and workflow work.
notes: Discovered while mapping the LaunchDeck proving-program docs under docs-topology-cleanup.
build-feature-catalog still generates output, but validate-feature-catalog fails with missing refs under docs-topology-cleanup, workflow-collaboration-surfaces, and autonomous-lane-control-plane.
New proving-program touch-set was kept aligned by updating .claude/catalog/feature-catalog.seed.json and re-running check-touch-set.ts, but the broader seed cleanup is still pending.

incidents.summary: Andy can emit a user-facing 'Dispatched' message for a request while the backing andy_requests row remains coordinator_active with no worker_run_id and no worker_runs row, creating a trust-breaking mismatch between visible status and actual execution state.
notes: Discovered during the first live LaunchDeck proving run on request req-1773692784606-5274h0tr for NAN-57 / LD-01.
User-facing evidence: Andy sent a 'Dispatched: NAN-57 LD-01' message naming jarvis-worker-1 and a branch.
Database evidence after the dispatch message: andy_requests row stayed state=coordinator_active with worker_run_id empty, and worker_runs had no row for request_id=req-1773692784606-5274h0tr.
Follow-up status query repeated the coordinator_active state ('Coordinator is processing your request'), confirming the visible dispatch message did not correspond to a linked worker run.

2026-03-17 fix landed in src/extensions/jarvis/user-facing-output.ts and src/index.ts: Andy dispatch-like output now waits for persisted worker-run confirmation before rendering a user-facing 'Dispatched ...' sentence, otherwise it falls back to truthful coordination text.
2026-03-17 regression coverage added in src/extensions/jarvis/user-facing-output.test.ts for confirmed, delayed, and unconfirmed dispatch states.

2026-03-17 live validation after restart: request req-1773693466333-pprimary stayed coordinator_active on immediate status reply, then linked to worker_run_id=req-1773693466333-pprimary before the worker stage advanced. This reproducer no longer showed the premature dispatch claim.

incidents.summary: Andy frontdesk status/progress replies remain flaky around restart/recovery windows: natural-language progress queries can be misclassified as new work, and immediate status replies can miss the 8s SLA when stale pending messages are being recovered.
notes: Discovered while running scripts/test-andy-full-user-journey-e2e.ts after the dispatch-truthfulness fix.
Live evidence on token 1773693464880-u8hef: '@Andy what is the current progress' created req-1773693529520-sstm8g7x instead of returning an immediate frontdesk progress reply.

Code hardening landed in src/extensions/jarvis/frontdesk-service.ts: frontdesk now handles pure greetings/status queries before intake ack, and the query matcher now recognizes 'what is the current progress' / 'what is the current status'.
Regression coverage added in src/extensions/jarvis/frontdesk-service.test.ts for the exact natural-language phrase that failed live.

A second live run immediately after service restart still missed the immediate status-reply SLA for req-1773693659172-pprimary; logs show recovery reopened stale pending Andy messages around startup, so restart/recovery interference is still unresolved.

2026-03-17 follow-up fix landed in src/index.ts: newly arrived Andy frontdesk queries are pre-handled before older pending work is merged, and already-processed messages are filtered out of the later allPending prompt batch.

2026-03-17 live verification improved materially: the full-user-journey build stage now passes intake ack, immediate status, dispatch linkage, immediate progress, worker terminal, and the post-worker status check.

incidents.summary: In multi-stage Andy journeys, stale control/retry messages from earlier requests can still bleed into later coordinator prompts, causing Andy to emit dispatch JSON for an older request while a newer request remains coordinator_active.
notes: Observed during the live full-user-journey run for token 1773694440374-38luj when stage two request req-1773694551674-eprimary was active but Andy emitted a dispatch payload for req-1773694442351-pprimary instead.

The bleed is narrower after filtering already-processed messages from allPending, but a later run still showed Andy outputting raw JSON for older retry request req-1773694737377-reretry2 while fresh build request req-1773694747631-pprimary was active.

Current hypothesis: pending-message hygiene around deferred review triggers and previously acknowledged retry messages is still insufficient, so older non-terminal control messages remain eligible for later coordinator prompts.
